purpose: Form modal for creating or editing a single task (agent) inside the SwarmBuilder before the swarm is launched. Captures title, wave (existing or '+ New Wave' with name input), layer, directory, dependencies (multi-select checkbox list of other agents), and description. On Save, returns a taskData object via onSave callback containing trimmed values, an _newWaveName field if the user picked '+ New Wave' (signal for parent to create the wave), and (in edit mode) the original id. The available-deps list excludes the agent currently being edited to prevent self-cycles.
gotchas: Only PREVENTS direct self-dependency (filter a.id !== editTask.id) — does NOT prevent indirect cycles. The SwarmBuilder/parent must validate the full dependency graph (e.g. via Kahn's algorithm in src/server/utils/validation.js).

When wave='__new__' is selected, the wave field on returned taskData is null (not 0/undefined) — parent handler must check _newWaveName presence to decide whether to create a new wave and assign its id.
If user selects '+ New Wave' but leaves the name input blank, defaults to 'New Wave' silently — no error or inline validation feedback.
directory defaults to '.' if blank after trim — meaning empty directory is interpreted as project root, NOT as 'unspecified'.
Save button is disabled when title is empty, but doesn't validate other fields. Description being empty is allowed (worker will receive an empty description prompt body).
selectedDeps is initialized from editTask.depends_on — if editTask.depends_on contains stale ids referencing deleted agents, those ids persist in selectedDeps until the user toggles them off.
patterns: Conditional UI: '+ New Wave' option in select toggles a follow-up name input field — common pattern for inline-creation in dropdowns.
Boolean isEdit derived from !!editTask drives initial state values.
conventions: Class names mix .task-editor-* with .settings-app-* (rows/labels/inputs reused from SettingsModal grid).
